remember: Students will be able to identify and explain the key features of the bipolar world order.
Students will be able to recall the major events and developments that led to the end of bipolarity.
Students will be able to list the key players involved in the collapse of the Soviet Union.
understand: Students will be able to explain the causes and consequences of the end of bipolarity.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of the end of bipolarity on the international system.
Students will be able to understand the role of the United States and the Soviet Union in the maintenance of the bipolar world order.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of bipolarity to explain the international relations during the Cold War era.
Students will be able to analyze the role of key events and actors in the end of bipolarity.
Students will be able to evaluate the implications of the end of bipolarity for the contemporary international system.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the complex factors that contributed to the end of bipolarity.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of the end of bipolarity on the global economy and security.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the international relations before and after the end of bipolarity.

ncert_objectives: Explain the factors that led to the end of bipolarity and the emergence of a unipolar world order.
Discuss the implications of the end of bipolarity for the international system and global governance.
Analyze the role of the United States in maintaining and ending the bipolar world order.
board_exam_objectives: What are the key features of the bipolar world order?
Explain the causes and consequences of the end of the Soviet Union.
Discuss the impact of the end of bipolarity on the international system and global governance.
